OWASP_TOP_10.name: Broken Access Control
OWASP_TOP_10.description: Failures typically lead to unauthorized information disclosure, modification, or destruction of all data.

OWASP_TOP_10.name: Cryptographic Failures
OWASP_TOP_10.description: Failures related to cryptography, which often leads to sensitive data exposure or system compromise.

OWASP_TOP_10.name: Injection
OWASP_TOP_10.description: User-supplied data is not validated, filtered, or sanitized by the application.

OWASP_TOP_10.name: Insecure Design
OWASP_TOP_10.description: Risks related to design flaws, requiring threat modeling, secure design patterns, and reference architectures.

OWASP_TOP_10.name: Security Misconfiguration
OWASP_TOP_10.description: Insecure default settings, incomplete configurations, open cloud storage, misconfigured HTTP headers.

OWASP_LLM_TOP_10.name: Prompt Injection
OWASP_LLM_TOP_10.description: Crafted prompts that trick the LLM into executing malicious commands.

OWASP_LLM_TOP_10.name: Insecure Output Handling
OWASP_LLM_TOP_10.description: Vulnerabilities occur when an LLM output is accepted without scrutiny.

OWASP_LLM_TOP_10.name: Training Data Poisoning
OWASP_LLM_TOP_10.description: Tampering with training data to introduce vulnerabilities or biases.

OWASP_LLM_TOP_10.name: Sensitive Information Disclosure
OWASP_LLM_TOP_10.description: LLM reveals sensitive information, proprietary algorithms, or confidential data.

OWASP_LLM_TOP_10.name: Excessive Agency
OWASP_LLM_TOP_10.description: LLM interfaces with other systems and executes unintended actions.
